title: 10 best herbal creams for eczema

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with eczema because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active ingredient, calendula oil, soothes and calms itchy skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with eczema. Calendula also has antioxidant properties that help to protect the skin from further damage, promoting healing and regeneration of damaged skin cells. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to lock in hydration, leaving skin feeling soft and supple.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Pot marigold contains flavonoids and terpenes that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with eczema.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in pot marigold cream can neutralize free radicals that damage skin cells, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that can exacerbate eczema symptoms.

Wound-healing and moisturizing compounds: Pot marigold contains triterpenoid saponins and other lipids that promote wound healing by enhancing collagen synthesis, improving skin hydration, and protecting the skin's natural barrier function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated skin.
Seeds: Containing fatty acids and essential oils, these seeds provide a natural emollient properties that help to moisturize and protect dry, itchy skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers at peak bloom and dry them thoroughly in a warm place or using a dehydrator for 24 hours.
Grind 20% dried calendula flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle to release their active compounds.
Melt 60 grams of beeswax and 30 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat, stirring until fully incorporated for 15 minutes.
Add the ground calendula powder to the melted wax and oil mixture, stirring well to combine, and let it infuse for an additional 10 minutes.
Remove the double boiler from heat, stir in 20 drops of vitamin e oil as a natural preservative, and pour the cream into sterilized containers for immediate use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with eczema because of its unique composition, rich in vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like calcium and potassium. The aloe vera gel has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with eczema. Additionally, aloe's moisturizing abilities help to hydrate the skin, reducing dryness and itchiness, which are common symptoms of eczema. This natural remedy provides long-lasting relief for those suffering from this chronic skin condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal aloe creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin, aloe-emodin, and chromone, which help reduce inflammation and itching associated with eczema.

Moisturizing properties: Aloe vera gel in herbal aloe creams has natural moisturizing properties that help soothe dry, itchy skin and lock in moisture, reducing the risk of further irritation and flare-ups.

Antioxidant activity: Herbal aloe creams often contain antioxidants like vitamins C and E, which help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, which help soothe and calm irritated skin.
Roots: Containing flavonoids and saponins that have antiallergic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective for treating eczema.
remedy_recipe: Sterilize equipment and workspace using a hot water bath or steam cleaner.
Grind 100g of dried chamomile flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix the ground chamomile with 50ml of distilled water to create a tea, then simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the cooled chamomile tea through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a bowl, discarding solids and retaining liquid.
Combine the strained chamomile liquid with 100g of aloe vera gel, 50g of coconut oil, and 25g of beeswax in a heat-proof container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with eczema because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The herb's flavonoids and terpenoids work together to reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with eczema. The cream's moisturizing effects also help to lock in hydration, calming the skin and reducing dryness that can exacerbate eczema symptoms. By providing a protective barrier against irritants and allergens, chamomile creams create an ideal environment for the skin to heal, leading to improved overall skin health and reduced eczema flares.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with eczema, providing relief from uncomfortable symptoms.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of chamomile creams help to combat oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate eczema symptoms and lead to skin thickening and scarring.

Soothing and Calming: Chamomile's natural soothing and calming effects help to reduce irritation and discomfort, promoting a sense of relaxation and reducing the risk of further skin irritation and allergic reactions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in soothing compounds that calm irritated skin and reduce inflammation associated with eczema.
Flowers: Contain apigenin, an antioxidant that helps to reduce redness and itching caused by eczema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers from a trusted source for optimal potency.
Combine the dried flowers with 200ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil at a ratio of 1:2.
Steep the mixture in a dark glass jar for 4-6 weeks, shaking daily to facilitate extraction and infusion.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding solids and retaining the liquid extract.
Blend the extract with 100g of beeswax and 50ml of coconut cream to create a smooth and creamy texture, heating gently until fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with eczema because they contain the natural anti-inflammatory compound, triterpenoids. These compounds have been shown to effectively soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with eczema. Additionally, the cream's antimicrobial properties help to prevent bacterial infections that can exacerbate eczema symptoms. The moisturizing properties of Kansas coneflower also help to lock in moisture, leaving skin feeling soft and supple. Overall, the unique combination of triterpenoids, antimicrobial, and moisturizing properties make herbal Kansas coneflower creams an effective treatment for managing eczema symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Kansas coneflower creams contain antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to inflammation and irritation associated with eczema.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: The creams' anti-inflammatory compounds, such as triterpenoids, help reduce redness, swelling, and itching by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory molecules and enzymes that exacerbate eczema symptoms.

Immunomodulatory Properties: Kansas coneflower creams contain immunomodulators that can modulate the immune response to prevent overactive or underactive immune responses, which can contribute to the development and worsening of eczema.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe itchy skin and reduce inflammation associated with eczema.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenoids, which have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help calm and heal dry, irritated skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry echinacea angustifolia roots to ensure optimal potency for the cream.
Combine dried echinacea angustifolia roots with sweet almond oil and beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax melts and the mixture reaches 160°f.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding the solids, and let it cool to 120°f.
Whip the cooled mixture with an electric mixer until it thickens and emulsifies, resulting in a creamy texture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with eczema because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The licorice root extract in these creams contains compounds like glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to reduce redness and swelling associated with eczema. Additionally, herbal licorice creams contain flavonoids that have antioxidant properties, helping to calm and protect the skin from further irritation. This combination of anti-inflammatory and antioxidant benefits makes licorice creams an effective treatment for soothing and calming eczema-prone skin.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory effects, which helps to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with eczema.
Flavonoids: These plant compounds have antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that can exacerbate eczema.

Saponins: These natural surfactants have moisturizing and emollient properties, which help to lock in moisture, reduce dryness, and soften the skin, making it more comfortable for individuals with eczema.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties and helps soothe skin irritation associated with eczema.
Barks: Licorice barks have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to reduce inflammation and itching in skin conditions like eczema due to its high concentration of triterpenoid saponins.
remedy_recipe: Grind 20 grams of dried licorice root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground licorice root powder with 100 milliliters of distilled water and let it steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove the solids and discard the solids.
Combine the resulting liquid extract with 200 grams of petroleum jelly or other emollient cream base and mix until well combined.
Pour the mixture into sterilized containers and allow it to cool and thicken before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with eczema because they possess soothing and anti-inflammatory properties that calm irritated skin. The mucilages present in marshmallows form a protective barrier on the skin's surface, shielding it from further irritation and reducing itching and scratching. Additionally, herbal marshmallow creams often contain other beneficial ingredients like chamomile and lavender, which further enhance their calming effects, allowing for improved skin hydration and reduced inflammation, ultimately providing relief to eczema sufferers.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Marshmallow creams contain mucilages, which are thick, protective substances that soothe and calm irritated skin by forming a barrier between the skin and external environment, reducing inflammation and itching.
Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in marshmallow creams help to neutralize free radicals, which can cause oxidative stress and exacerbate eczema symptoms such as redness, swelling, and dryness.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Marshmallow creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate itching, redness, and swelling associated with eczema.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and calms irritated skin, making them ideal for treating eczema.
Buds: The buds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling associated with eczema, promoting healing and reducing discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry sufficient quantity of marshmallow root aethaea officinalis for future use.
Grind dried marshmallow root into fine powder using mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Combine 20 grams of ground marshmallow root with 100 milliliters of distilled water in a heat-resistant bowl.
Steep mixture at low heat for 30 minutes to create a decoction, then strain through cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve.
Mix cooled decoction with 200 grams of petroleum jelly and 10 grams of beeswax in a double boiler until fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm creams helps with eczema because of its unique ability to soothe and calm irritated skin. The mucilages present in slippery elm create a protective barrier on the skin's surface, locking in moisture and reducing inflammation. This natural emollient also helps to reduce itching and scratching, allowing the skin to heal and regenerate more effectively. Additionally, slippery elm's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce redness and swelling, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving eczema symptoms.
remedy_properties: Mucoadhesive and emollient properties: Slippery elm's mucilage coats and soothes irritated skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort, while its emollient properties moisturize and hydrate the skin.

Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties: Slippery elm contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the skin, helping to alleviate eczema symptoms such as redness, itching, and swelling.

Soothing and protective barrier function: Slippery elm's mucilage creates a physical barrier on the skin's surface, protecting it from further irritation and environmental factors that can exacerbate eczema, while its anti-inflammatory properties help soothe and calm irritated skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, a natural thickening agent that soothes and protects the skin from inflammation and irritation.
Barks: Contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness and itching associated with eczema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried ulmus rubra bark in amounts of 20 grams per serving.
Grind the dried ulmus rubra bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground ulmus rubra bark powder with beeswax and coconut oil in a 1:4 ratio.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes, stirring frequently, to melt the beeswax.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a glass jar and allow it to cool before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock creams helps with eczema because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The cream's rich antioxidant content also helps to reduce redness and swelling, while its moisturizing agents lock in hydration and support the skin's natural barrier function. Additionally, burdock root has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, helping to combat fungal and bacterial infections that can exacerbate eczema symptoms, making it a valuable natural remedy for managing this chronic skin condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Burdock cream's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with eczema, providing relief to affected skin areas.
Antioxidant: Burdock's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eczema flare-ups and dryness.

Soothing and moisturizing: Burdock cream's soothing and moisturizing properties help calm irritated skin, lock in moisture, and promote a healthy skin barrier function, reducing symptoms of eczema such as dryness and tightness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in inulin, a prebiotic fiber that soothes and calms irritated skin.
Seeds: High in linoleic acid, an omega-6 fatty acid that helps to reduce inflammation and promote skin health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh arctium lappa roots and wash them thoroughly with cool water.
Dry the roots at room temperature for 7-10 days or by air drying for 2-3 weeks.
Grind dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 20% arctium lappa powder with 80% coconut oil or other carrier oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture under low heat for 30-40 minutes, stirring occasionally, until thickened and smooth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with eczema because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant's extract, rich in vitamins A, C, and E, soothes and calms irritated skin, reducing redness and itching associated with eczema. Additionally, dandelion's natural astringent and antiseptic properties help to shrink pores, reduce acne, and prevent bacterial infections that can exacerbate eczema symptoms. By providing long-lasting relief from inflammation and discomfort, dandelion creams offer a natural and effective solution for managing mild to moderate eczema cases.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Dandelion cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with eczema by soothing and calming the skin.
Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in dandelion cream help neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate eczema symptoms, reducing damage to the skin and promoting healthy healing.
Emollient and moisturizing: Dandelion cream's emollient properties help lock in moisture, soothe dryness, and calm irritation, making it an effective treatment for eczema-prone skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Dandelion roots contain inulin, a natural prebiotic that helps soothe and calm irritated skin, making it effective for treating eczema.
Fruits: Dandelion fruits are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and itching associated with eczema.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of dried taraxacum officinale leaves and stems and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered taraxacum officinale with 200ml of cold-pressed coconut oil in a heat-resistant glass beaker.
Place the mixture in a double boiler or a makeshift one created by placing a heat-proof bowl over a pot of simmering water and stir until it reaches 60°c after 30 minutes.
Allow the mixture to steep for an additional 1 hour before removing it from the heat and straining it through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar.
Add 10ml of beeswax and 2ml of vitamin e oil to the filtered infusion and stir until the beeswax is fully melted and the cream has reached a smooth consistency after 15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain creams helps with eczema because of its potent anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The plantain herb, also known as "plantago major," has been used for centuries to calm irritated skin and reduce redness. When applied topically, the cream helps to reduce itching and inflammation associated with eczema, allowing the skin to heal naturally. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to lock in moisture and soothe dry, itchy skin, providing long-lasting relief from eczema symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain creams contain compounds like aucuba and berberine, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with eczema, providing relief to affected skin.

Sothistic and protective: The mucilages present in plantain cream create a soothing barrier on the skin's surface, reducing irritation and protecting it from further damage caused by environmental factors or allergens, helping to calm and heal irritated skin.

Antioxidant: Plantain contains antioxidants like vitamin C and flavonoids that help neutralize free radicals, which can exacerbate eczema symptoms by causing oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting a healthier and more resilient skin environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: Plantain roots have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated skin.
Barks: The barks of the plantain plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which provide antibacterial and antifungal properties that help to heal and protect eczema-prone skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh plantago major leaves and chop them into small pieces ensuring about 20g of leaf material.
Steep the chopped plant material in 200ml of boiling distilled water for 10-15 minutes allowing it to infuse.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container discarding solids and retaining liquid.
Combine the resulting herbal extract with 100g of melted beeswax and 50g of coconut oil in a heat-resistant bowl stirring until fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into sterilized cream jars allowing it to cool and set at room temperature for about an hour before sealing tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder creams helps with eczema because of their unique blend of natural ingredients that soothe and calm irritated skin. The anti-inflammatory properties of elderflowers, for example, help reduce redness and swelling, while the emollient properties of beeswax and coconut oil moisturize and soften dry, cracked skin. Additionally, the antioxidants present in elder creams can help repair damaged skin cells and promote healthy skin regeneration, reducing the appearance of eczema symptoms such as itchiness, rashes, and discoloration.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Elder cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthocyanins, which help reduce inflammation and itching associated with eczema.

Antioxidant activity: Elder cream's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate skin damage and irritation caused by eczema, promoting healing and reducing oxidative stress.

Soothing and protective barrier formation: The natural oils and waxes present in elder cream create a soothing and protective barrier on the skin, helping to calm inflammation, reduce moisture loss, and prevent further irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing irritated skin and reducing inflammation associated with eczema.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce oxidative stress and improve skin hydration, calming dry and itchy skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried sambucus nigra flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the powdered sambucus nigra with 50ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a glass jar and stir well.
Infuse the mixture by placing it in a warm water bath at 60°c for 2 hours to allow the herbs to extract their active compounds.
Strain the infused mixture through a cheesecloth into another glass jar, squeezing the cheesecloth to extract as much oil as possible before discarding the solids.
Allow the cream to mature for 48 hours before using it on affected skin areas, stirring well and applying thinly to avoid clogging pores.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams help with eczema because of their anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce redness and itching associated with this skin condition. The antioxidants present in ginger also help to soothe and calm irritated skin, promoting healing and reducing the risk of infection. Additionally, ginger's natural oils have moisturizing properties that can help to hydrate and soften dry, itchy skin, providing relief from discomfort and promoting a sense of relaxation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation in the skin, which can alleviate symptoms of eczema like redness, itching, and swelling.

Antioxidant properties: Ginger's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to skin cells, which can contribute to the development of eczema lesions.
Methoxyflavones: Ginger contains methoxyflavones, which have been shown to reduce histamine levels in the skin, helping to alleviate itching and scratching associated with eczema.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, or underground stem, is a rich source of active compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated skin affected by eczema.

Roots: Ginger roots contain bioactive compounds like gingerone and paradol, which possess antipruritic and anti-oxidant activities, making them effective in reducing itching and inflammation associated with eczema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality zingiber officinale roots and wash them thoroughly with distilled water.
Dry the cleaned roots in a warm dark place for at least 7 days or until they become brittle.
Grind the dried ginger roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground ginger powder with 20% coconut oil and 80% distilled water to create a paste, heating it gently over low heat for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into sterilized glass jars, allowing it to cool before storage and use within 2 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with eczema because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling associated with this chronic skin condition. The active compound curcumin in turmeric has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory molecules, thus calming irritated skin and promoting a more balanced immune response. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate eczema symptoms, leading to improved skin health and reduced discomfort.
remedy_properties: Curcuminoids: Curcuminoids, particularly curcumin, have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of eczema such as redness, itching, and swelling.

Corticosteroid-like effects: Turmeric contains a compound called gingerol, which has been shown to exhibit corticosteroid-like effects, helping to reduce inflammation and relieve symptoms of eczema without the side effects associated with synthetic steroids.
Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial properties of turmeric help to prevent bacterial overgrowth and infection in skin lesions, reducing the risk of complications and promoting faster healing.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Turmeric rhyzomes are rich in curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and calm eczema symptoms.

Roots: The roots of the turmeric plant contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, including curcumin, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and improve skin health for eczema sufferers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality turmeric roots and dry them thoroughly for at least two weeks.
Grind dried turmeric roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder.
Mix 10 grams of the turmeric powder with 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a temperature of 120°f to 130°f.
Strain the cream through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve and transfer it to an airtight container for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oats creams helps with eczema because of their unique ability to soothe and calm irritated skin. The natural astringent and anti-inflammatory properties in oats help to reduce redness and swelling, while also providing long-lasting moisturization. This gentle cream works by forming a protective barrier on the skin's surface, allowing it to heal and repair itself. Additionally, the antioxidants present in herbal oat creams further enhance their therapeutic benefits, making them an effective treatment option for alleviating eczema symptoms.
remedy_properties: Soothing anti-inflammatory effects: Herbal oats contain avenanthramides, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and relieve itching associated with eczema, providing quick relief for irritated skin.

Moisturizing and protective barrier function: Oats contain beta-glucans, which help to lock in moisture and create a physical barrier on the skin's surface, shielding it from environmental irritants and allergens that can exacerbate eczema.

Antioxidant and antimicrobial properties: Herbal oats have antioxidant compounds like vitamin E and flavonoids, which help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to inflammation and oxidative stress in eczema-prone skin, while also inhibiting the growth of bacteria and fungi that can infect broken skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties, making them effective in soothing dry and itchy skin.
Rhyzomes: Contain a high concentration of beta-glucans, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing.
remedy_recipe: Grind 2 tablespoons of dried oatmeal into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered oatmeal with 1/4 cup of beeswax pellets and 1 tablespoon of sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches a temperature of 160°f to 180°f.
Remove the double boiler from the heat and let it cool slightly before adding 2 teaspoons of dried avena sativa herb infused in 1 tablespoon of coconut oil.
Stir well and pour the cream into sterilized jars, allowing it to cool and solidify completely at room temperature before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Beech creams helps with eczema because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe itchy skin, reduce redness and swelling. The natural ingredients in beech creams, such as salicylic acid and glycyrrhizin, have been shown to calm irritated skin and promote healing. Additionally, the moisturizing properties of beech creams help to lock in moisture, reducing dryness and flakiness that can exacerbate eczema symptoms. As a result, beech creams provide long-lasting relief for itchy, inflamed skin, offering a natural solution for managing eczema.
remedy_properties: Emollients: Beechnut cream's emollient properties help to soothe and calm dry, itchy skin by locking in moisture and reducing inflammation, providing relief from eczema symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in beechnut cream, such as salicin and betulin, help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with eczema by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals in the body.

Antioxidants: Beechnut cream's rich antioxidant content helps to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eczema symptoms like dryness, irritation, and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help to calm irritated skin and reduce redness.
Bark: Contains a high concentration of salicin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce itching and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fagus sylvatica leaves and clean them thoroughly with distilled water.
Dry the leaves in a low-temperature oven at 60°c for 2 hours.
Combine 20g of dried leaves with 100ml of coconut oil and simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard solids, then add 10% beeswax by weight.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for 2 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey creams helps with eczema because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The herb's allantoin content reduces skin irritation and redness, while its mucilages form a protective barrier on the skin, locking in moisture and reducing dryness. This combination provides quick relief from itching and discomfort associated with eczema, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of infection. Additionally, comfrey's antioxidant properties help to combat free radicals that can exacerbate skin conditions, making it an effective natural treatment for eczema sufferers.
remedy_properties: Allantoin: Comfrey cream contains allantoin, a natural compound that soothes and calms irritated skin, reducing inflammation and promoting faster healing in eczema-prone areas.

Rutin: Rutin, a flavonoid found in comfrey, has potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with eczema, while also improving blood vessel circulation to affected areas.

Hormonal regulation: Comfrey's hormonal-regulating properties can help balance the body's natural hormone production, which is often disrupted in individuals with eczema, leading to reduced inflammation and improved skin barrier function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Comfrey roots are rich in allantoin, a natural substance that promotes skin regeneration and helps to soothe and calm irritated skin.
Buds: The buds of the comfrey plant contain high levels of rosmarinic acid, an antioxidant that helps to reduce inflammation and itching associated with eczema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 500g of fresh symphytum officinale leaves in a warm dark place for 7-10 days.
Combine the dried herbs with 200ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 6 weeks, shaking the jar every other day to facilitate infusion.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.
Whip the infused oil with 20g of beeswax and 10g of shea butter in a double boiler until it reaches a smooth creamy consistency after 30 minutes preparation time.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with eczema because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's valerian root extract calms irritated skin, reducing redness and itching associated with eczema. It also helps to lock in moisture, which is often lacking in dry, itchy skin affected by eczema. By promoting relaxation and reducing stress, the cream can also help alleviate the emotional distress that often accompanies this chronic condition. As a natural and gentle treatment, valerian creams offer a safe and effective solution for managing eczema symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Valerian cream reduces inflammation, which helps to alleviate redness, swelling, and itching associated with eczema.
Antioxidant properties: Valerian's antioxidants help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eczema flare-ups.
Soothing and calming effects: The cream's gentle, non-irritating nature helps to calm and soothe itchy and irritated skin, providing relief from discomfort and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are rich in essential oils, particularly sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help calm skin irritations associated with eczema.

Rhyzomes: Valerian rhyzomes contain a higher concentration of alkaloids and volatile oils compared to the roots, making them an effective ingredient in creams for reducing inflammation and itching caused by eczema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest valeriana officinalis roots and clean them thoroughly by rinsing with distilled water and removing any debris.
Dry the cleaned valeriana officinalis roots in a warm oven at 150°f for 24 hours or using a dehydrator according to manufacturer's instructions.
Grind the dried valeriana officinalis roots into a fine powder using an electric grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered valeriana officinalis with 20% beeswax, 10% coconut oil, and 70% distilled water in a double boiler and heat until melted and mixed thoroughly.
Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature before whipping into a creamy consistency using an electric mixer or spatula.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sassafras creams helps with eczema because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which provide relief from itchy and irritated skin. The cream's active ingredients, such as saponins and terpenoids, help to calm the skin's natural response to allergens or irritants, reducing redness and swelling. Additionally, sassafras creams can help to lock in moisture, leaving the skin feeling soft and supple. This makes it an effective treatment option for people suffering from eczema-prone skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Sassafras contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with eczema.
Antioxidant: Sassafras has antioxidant properties, which can help neutralize free radicals and protect the skin from damage caused by oxidative stress, a common contributor to eczema.
Antipruritic: The alkaloids present in sassafras have been shown to have antipruritic effects, meaning they can help relieve itching and scratching sensations that are characteristic of eczema.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing a rich source of bioactive compounds, including safrole and myristicin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe and calm eczema-prone skin.
Barks: Rich in terpenoids and flavonoids, sassafras barks have been traditionally used to treat skin conditions like eczema due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality sassafras albidum roots and extract their essential oil using steam distillation for 2 hours.
Mix the extracted oil with 20% beeswax, 30% coconut oil, and 50% shea butter in a double boiler for 15 minutes.
Add 10 grams of dried calendula flowers to the mixture and stir well for an additional 5 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool for 1 hour before whipping it into a smooth cream using an electric mixer.
Pour the finished cream into sterilized jars, seal tightly, and store in a cool, dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with eczema because of its unique composition, rich in antioxidants and fatty acids. The cream's ability to soothe and calm irritated skin reduces inflammation, relieving itching and discomfort associated with eczema. Additionally, the moisturizing properties of maidenhair tree extracts help repair the skin's natural barrier, reducing dryness and flaking. As a result, the creams provide effective relief from eczema symptoms, promoting healthy-looking skin and improving overall skin health.
remedy_properties: Saponins: Saponins have anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with eczema.
Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, which can help combat bacterial and fungal infections that exacerbate eczema while also reducing oxidative stress.
Polyphenols: Polyphenols have anti-inflammatory and antiallergic effects, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as itching, redness, and blistering caused by eczema.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing redness and swelling associated with eczema.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids that help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing itching and discomfort caused by eczema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves from a trusted supplier or local herb store for extraction.
Combine the dried ginkgo biloba leaves with 200 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar and let it steep for 14 days.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container to separate the plant material from the tincture.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax, 10 grams of coconut oil, and 5 milliliters of the ginkgo biloba tincture with 50 milliliters of distilled water in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat, stir until it reaches a smooth consistency, and pour into sterilized glass jars to cool and harden completely within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with eczema because they provide a soothing, anti-inflammatory solution for irritated skin. The cream's active ingredients, such as beta-carotene and fatty acids, help to reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with eczema. Additionally, stinging nettle's natural antihistamine properties can ease the allergic responses that often exacerbate eczema. As a result, stinging nettle creams can bring relief and comfort to individuals suffering from this chronic skin condition, promoting a smoother, healthier complexion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Stinging nettle creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like caffeic acid, quercetin, and gallic acid, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of eczema such as redness and swelling.
Antioxidant properties: Nettle cream's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate eczema and promote healing.
Soothing and moisturizing effects: The cream's moisturizing properties help soothe dry, itchy skin, while its soothing effects calm irritated skin and reduce discomfort associated with eczema.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective at soothing and calming skin irritations associated with eczema.
Barks: Contain antioxidants and flavonoids that help reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, making them beneficial for eczema treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh nettle leaves from a pesticide-free area in early spring for optimal potency.
Dry the nettle leaves in a warm, dark place for 1-2 weeks or using a food dehydrator on low setting.
Combine 100g of dried nettle leaves with 200ml of olive oil and 20g of beeswax in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean container, discarding solids after 10-15 minutes of pressing time.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken at room temperature for 2-3 hours before transferring to sterilized jars for storage.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for eczema are topical preparations made from natural ingredients, such as herbs, oils, and botanicals, that provide relief from the symptoms of eczema. These creams help to soothe and calm dry, itchy, and inflamed skin by reducing inflammation and promoting moisture retention. Examples of herbal creams that help with eczema include chamomile, lavender, and calendula creams, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Using these creams can improve lives by providing long-lasting relief from itching and discomfort, allowing individuals to feel more confident and comfortable in their own skin.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat eczema with herbal creams is to look for products containing natural ingredients such as aloe vera, chamomile, and calendula. These herbs have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help reduce redness and itching associated with eczema. Apply the cream topically to affected areas several times a day, or as directed by the product label, for effective relief from symptoms.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to eczema that are treated with herbal creams are psoriasis, rosacea, and dermatitis. Herbal creams containing ingredients like aloe vera, calendula, and tea tree oil have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe and calm skin irritations, reducing redness and itching. They also promote healthy skin regeneration and improve the appearance of skin lesions. These natural remedies can provide effective relief from chronic skin conditions without harsh chemicals or side effects.
